---
title: "Analyzing Cosmetics Data (CSV) - Solutions"
author: "Charlie Lindgren"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```

## Solutions

### Exercise 1: Load the Data

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
cosmetics <- read_csv(
  "working_with_csv/_data/most_used_beauty_cosmetics_products_extended.csv"
)
```

### Exercise 2: Explore the Data

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
str(cosmetics)
summary(cosmetics)
unique(cosmetics$Product_Size)
glimpse(cosmetics) # Using dplyr's glimpse for a compact view
```

### Exercise 3: Data Transformation - Product Size

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
cosmetics$Product_Size <- factor(cosmetics$Product_Size,
  levels = c("30ml", "50ml", "100ml", "150ml", "200ml", "250ml"),
  ordered = TRUE
)
# Display the new column structure
levels(cosmetics$Product_Size)
str(cosmetics$Product_Size)
```

### Exercise 4: Data Filtering

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
cosmetics_large_size <- cosmetics %>%
  filter(Product_Size > "150ml")
print(head(cosmetics_large_size))
```

### Exercise 5: Data Summarization

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
summary(cosmetics_large_size)
nrow(cosmetics_large_size)
```

### Exercise 6: Data Visualization - Price Distribution

```{r, eval=TRUE, echo=TRUE}
ggplot(cosmetics, aes(x = Price_USD)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 10, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Product Prices (USD)",
    x = "Price (USD)",
    y = "Frequency"
  )
```
